goal: Close remaining OCR gaps: runtime dependencies, scanned PDF page-level OCR, and Settings OCR status management.
assumptions: OCR remains config gated outside Docker Compose.
Docker Compose may enable OCR because the image installs Tesseract and Poppler.
PDF OCR v1 uses pdftoppm with a bounded OCR_PDF_MAX_PAGES limit.
Missing OCR dependencies must be observable and non-fatal.
risks: OCR dependencies increase container image size.
OCR on scanned PDFs can be CPU-heavy without a page limit.
Status UI must not expose secrets or worker internals.
steps: Add failing backend tests for Docker packages, PDF conversion, dependency fallback, and OCR status endpoint.
Add failing frontend test for Settings OCR status readiness.
Implement OCR PDF config and page-limited Poppler conversion.
Add OCR status API and Settings status card.
Update Docker/Compose, constraint docs, quality score, and run verification.
acceptance: Dockerfile includes Tesseract and Poppler packages.
PDF OCR renders only configured pages and indexes page text.
Settings shows OCR enabled state, dependency readiness, and PDF page limit.
